text: For gastric cardiovascularity, intravenous injection is used.
Bilateral superficial inguinal lymph nodes are identified using USG guidance and ICG is injected 1 ml each.
Patient is position prone and infrasagus dissection is started anteriorly first, allowing the esophagus to hang from the roof.
The inferior pulmonary ligament is divided and the dissection is done up to the cross of diaphragm and lower GE junction.
Subcarinal lymph node is carefully dissected to avoid thermal injury to right and left main bronchus.
Dissection is then done posteriorly and thoracic duct is identified using ICG Firefly technology.
Under fluorescent guidance, complete infrasiagus esophageal dissection is done.
Dissection is then commenced in supra-azygous region.
Right paratracheal lymph nodes are dissected with careful preservation of right recurrent laryngeal nerve and clearance of station 106 lymph nodes.
Thereafter, we start our dissection on left side with identification and dissection of left recurrent laryngeal nerve.
The thoracic duct line close proximity to left recurrent laryngeal nerve in this region and ICG fluorescence helps identify and preserve and avoid injury to the duct.
lymph nodes over the subclavian vessels are dissected.
Slow and careful dissection is warranted in suprazygous region to avoid thermal injury to the membranous trachea.
Aziagus vein is clipped and divided.
Intact thoracic duct is confirmed and mobilization of intrathoracic esophagus is completed.
Passion is positioned supine and interabdominal dissection is started.
The lesser momentum incised and dissection done up to the cross of diaphragm.
Gastrosplenic ligament containing the short gastric vessel is divided using vessel sealer.
Periodic capsule is opened and station 8 and 12 lymph nodes are dissected.
The left gastric vessels are sealed and divided at the origin.
D2 dissection is completed with clearance of station 11P and 11D lymph nodes.
The greater momentum is last divided with careful preservation of the gastroepiploic arcade.
The gastric conduit is made using shore-form stapler.
3 ml of dye is injected intravenously and gastric condyovascularity is confirmed.
Side quadrangle esophagogastric anastomosis is performed in the neck using powered stapler.
